title: Whole-Body Bone Scintigraphy Research
subtitle: From annotation to thesis: a 3-year research arc

highlights: Got assigned as lead coder on a Ministry of Higher Education-funded project. No prior WBBS experience.
Annotated 600+ bone scan images in IbisPaintX — every skeletal region manually, supervised by nuclear medicine physicians at Dr. Hasan Sadikin General Hospital, Universitas Padjadjaran.
Wrote the detection and segmentation code: YOLOv8 for hotspot localization (mAP@0.5 = 0.599), nnU-Net v2 across 12 skeletal regions (DSC = 0.934).
3,000 lines of Python for BSI quantification. Patient-level AUC hit 0.956 — above EXINI and BONENAVI, both commercial systems.
Started visiting the hospital directly. Worked with nuclear medicine doctors at Dr. Hasan Sadikin to understand how BSI is actually scored in the clinic.
Grew the dataset from 600 to 3,593 annotated images — anterior and posterior views, 12 regions each.
Participated in building Telplastina v1, the first time this pipeline became an actual application.
Submitted the first preprint to SSRN in January 2025.
Turned 2 years of research into my bachelor's thesis.
Led development of Telplastina v2 — full stack: Vue 3 frontend, Rust backend, Docker deployment.
Journal paper accepted in IJIES-INASS. Dataset of 3,593 images released on Zenodo — open to anyone.
The pipeline now surpasses EXINI (AUC 0.83–0.88) and BONENAVI (0.84–0.89) on patient-level scoring.
